title: Campaign Strategy Route
subtitle: Research-tool launch campaign demo
reader_question: How does the campaign move from audience insight to measurable adoption?

stages.title: Audience
stages.summary: Identify who the campaign must persuade.

nodes.label: Map user segments
nodes.detail: Target graduate students, lab managers, and academic teams with different workflow needs.

stages.title: Insight
stages.summary: Find the trust driver behind adoption.

nodes.label: Lead with editability
nodes.detail: Users trust outputs more when visuals remain editable and evidence-linked.

nodes.label: Position workflow value
nodes.detail: Frame the product as agent-assisted source-to-editable-diagram workflow.

nodes.label: Show before and after
nodes.detail: Use problem hook, evidence-first promise, demo output, and use-case proof.

stages.title: Channels
stages.summary: Distribute the message through proof-oriented channels.

nodes.label: Use GitHub as hub
nodes.detail: Anchor the campaign in the README, examples, templates, and visible demo outputs.

nodes.label: Publish demo content
nodes.detail: Share short video, social posts, lab demo deck, and community templates.

stages.title: Measurement
stages.summary: Connect channel activity to adoption signals.

nodes.label: Track adoption signals
nodes.detail: Measure GitHub stars, demo runs, template downloads, issue feedback, and trials.

nodes.label: Optimize message route
nodes.detail: Refine messaging based on drop-off, issue themes, and output-quality feedback.
